mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and broad economic activity, which compounds to produce sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP and retail sales shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10), or forward returns are negative in >55% of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and manufacturing output growth, which translates into short-term positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside post-shock recovery periods, or 5-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 270.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.0, or the threshold shows no incremental predictive value over a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in >58% of triggered observations over the 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or returns are negative in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 98.0 indicates a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of instances when INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the threshold condition shows no statistical edge in walk-forward testing.
